macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Dow hits record high as U.S.-Iran talks dominate markets
items.summary: U.S. stocks climbed, with the Dow setting a record as investors tracked developments in U.S.-Iran negotiations. The move extended a strong run for equities even as geopolitical uncertainty remained a key market driver.

items.title: Wall Street keeps rising despite weaker household sentiment
items.summary: Markets continued to advance even as U.S. households showed growing discouragement, highlighting a disconnect between asset prices and consumer confidence. The commentary points to resilient equities amid mixed macro signals.

items.title: Dow hits record, S&P 500 logs eighth straight winning week
items.summary: U.S. equities extended gains, with the Dow reaching a fresh record and the S&P 500 posting an eighth consecutive weekly advance. The rally underscores strong momentum in risk assets despite ongoing policy and macro concerns.

items.title: New Fed chief to be sworn in at White House
items.summary: A new Federal Reserve chief is set to be sworn in at the White House, a notable development for U.S. monetary policy leadership. Markets are watching for any implications for the Fed’s interest-rate path and broader central bank stance.

items.title: Japan core consumer prices rise 1.4% in April, slowing after subsidy effects
items.summary: Japan’s inflation data showed core consumer prices rising 1.4% in April, with the pace moderated by policy measures such as free school lunches. The reading adds to the Bank of Japan’s ongoing challenge of balancing inflation persistence with policy normalization.

items.title: UN warns global growth will slow as Middle East conflict adds a new economic shock
items.summary: The UN said the Middle East crisis is threatening global growth and adding fresh pressure to the world economy. The warning highlights risks for trade, energy prices, and inflation if the conflict continues to escalate.

items.title: G7 finance ministers seek common ground amid divisions over Russia and trade
items.summary: G7 officials met to coordinate on trade and Russia-related issues, but differences remained over how aggressively to respond. The talks matter for currency markets, sanctions policy, and the broader global economic outlook.

items.title: Mofcom says China-U.S. trade talks produced preliminary outcomes on tariffs and trade issues
items.summary: China’s commerce ministry outlined preliminary results from economic and trade consultations with the U.S., including tariff-related matters. The update suggests continued negotiation, though major disputes remain unresolved.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI launches Deployment Company to help enterprises scale AI
items.summary: OpenAI has introduced a new business focused on helping enterprises move AI systems from pilots to production across critical workflows. The announcement targets organizations that need support with building, testing, and deploying AI at scale.
items.source: ODSC AI and Data Science News

items.title: EU lawmakers reach deal to delay parts of the AI Act
items.summary: EU countries and European Parliament lawmakers have reached a provisional deal to delay some high-risk AI compliance provisions, giving companies more time to prepare. Rules related to AI-generated explicit content and watermarking are expected to move forward sooner.
items.source: ODSC AI and Data Science News

items.title: Pennsylvania sues Character.AI over alleged medical chatbot claims
items.summary: Pennsylvania filed a lawsuit against Character Technologies, alleging that some Character.AI chatbots misled users by presenting themselves as licensed medical professionals. The case underscores growing scrutiny of consumer AI chatbots and their safety claims.
items.source: ODSC AI and Data Science News

items.title: BMW pilots AI models at battery cell center to improve production quality
items.summary: BMW is testing AI models at its Battery Cell Competence Centre in Munich to reduce trial-and-error testing and predict production outcomes earlier. The pilot is aimed at improving quality control in battery development and manufacturing.
items.source: ODSC AI and Data Science News

items.title: Cerebras targets $3.5 billion IPO amid surging AI chip demand
items.summary: AI chipmaker Cerebras is aiming for a Nasdaq listing as demand for specialized AI hardware continues to rise. The planned IPO highlights continued investor interest in AI infrastructure companies.
items.source: ODSC AI and Data Science News

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie reportedly facing significant layoffs, Destiny 3 not in development
items.summary: Multiple reports say Bungie is preparing layoffs after ending development on Destiny 2, with Destiny 3 reportedly not in development. The news has sparked major concern across the gaming industry and among Destiny fans.

items.title: Xbox game tops Steam charts, beating Subnautica 2 and LEGO Batman
items.summary: A new Xbox-published title has climbed to the top of Steam’s best-sellers list, overtaking Subnautica 2 and LEGO Batman. The ranking shift highlights how quickly player interest can swing around major releases and wishlists.

items.title: Capcom reportedly remaking an iconic PS1-era game
items.summary: Reports suggest Capcom is working on a remake of a classic PlayStation 1 game, fueling speculation about which franchise could be next in the publisher’s remake strategy. No official announcement has been made yet.

items.title: Major PS5 security vulnerability exposed
items.summary: A newly reported PS5 security issue is being described as a major vulnerability affecting users. Details remain limited, but the report has prompted concern about platform security.

items.title: Elden Ring free overhaul update adds a new enemy
items.summary: A free overhaul for Elden Ring received a substantial update that includes a new enemy encounter. The patch adds fresh content for players revisiting the game.

items.title: Nintendo ramps up Switch 2 production despite global price hike
items.summary: Nintendo is reportedly increasing Switch 2 production even as prices rise in markets around the world. The move suggests the company is preparing for strong demand ahead of wider rollout.

items.title: Helldivers 2 teases next warbond tied to Warhammer 40K
items.summary: Helldivers 2 is preparing its next major Warbond, and reports say it will be themed around Warhammer 40,000. Players should not expect Space Marines, though the crossover is still drawing attention.

items.title: No GTA 6 delay, still set for November launch
items.summary: Recent coverage says Grand Theft Auto 6 has not been delayed again and remains on track for a November release. The clarification comes amid persistent speculation about the game's schedule.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
